{"author": null, "closed_at": null, "collaborating_teams": null, "collaborating_users": null, "created_at": null, "credits": [], "credits_detailed": [], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-45153", "cvss": {"score": 4.6, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:P/AC:H/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:L/A:N"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 4.6, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:P/AC:H/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:L/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}}, "cwe_ids": ["CWE-287"], "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-287", "name": "Improper Authentication"}], "description": "### Impact

After unlocking a locked Android phone the back-button could be used to bypass the Nextcloud Files app PIN.

### Patches

It is recommended that the Nextcloud Android Files app is upgraded to 33.1.0

### Workarounds
* No workaround available

### References

<!-- Link to the main/master PR only -->

* [HackerOne](https://hackerone.com/reports/3625210)
* [PullRequest](https://github.com/nextcloud/android/pull/16896)

### For more information

If you have any questions or comments about this advisory:

* Create a post in [nextcloud/security-advisories](https://github.com/nextcloud/security-advisories/discussions)

title: PIN bypass in PassCodeActivity via back button
